ocr.text: In the analysis of lipid classes, Table 1 presents the following data: for TAG, the Chemical Shift is 4.34 ppm, with n being 2, the
Chemical Assignment as methylene protons of glycerol; for PL + GL, the Chemical Shift is in the range of 4.53–4.38 ppm, n is
1, and the Chemical Assignment is methylene protons of glycerol; for MGDG, the Chemical Shift is 3.88 ppm, n is 1, and the
Chemical Assignment is the methine proton at C4 of galactose; for DGDG, the Chemical Shift is 4.90 ppm, n is 1, with the
Chemical Assignment as the anomeric proton of galactose; for SQDG, the Chemical Shift is 4.80 ppm, n is 1, and the Chemical
Assignment is the anomeric proton of sulfoquinovoside; for TFA, the Chemical Shift is 2.35 a ppm, n is 2, with the Chemical
Assignment being methylene protons α to carboxy group; finally, for UFA, the Chemical Shift is 2.06 b ppm, n is 4, with the
Chemical Assignment as allylic protons.
S  Chemical Shift (ppm)  n  Chemical Assignment  Lipid Class
1  4.34  2  methylene protons of glycerol  TAG
2  4.53–4.38  1  methylene protons of glycerol  PL + GL
3  3.88  1  methine proton at C4 of galactose  MGDG
4  4.90  1  anomeric proton of galactose  DGDG
5  4.80  1  anomeric proton of sulfoquinovoside  SQDG
6  2.35 a  2  methylene protons α to carboxy group  TFA
7  2.06 b  4  allylic protons  UFA
Corneal disease  Rural area  Urban area  Total  Prevalence
No.  No.  No.  %
Pterygium  126  13  139  65.9%
Infectious keratitis  28  11  39  18.5%
Traumatic scar  10  3  13  6.1%
Unknown cause scars  11  9  20  9.5%
Table 2 details corneal diseases, showing Pterygium with 126 cases in Rural area and 13 in Urban area for a Total of 139 cases,
with a Prevalence of 65.9%; Infectious keratitis with 28 in Rural area and 11 in Urban area for a Total of 39 cases, with a
Prevalence of 18.5%; Traumatic scar has 10 in Rural area and 3 in Urban area for a Total of 13 cases, with a Prevalence of
